steps.title: Use the source clue
steps.content: The passage explains why Siemowit's existence is debated: knowledge of him comes from somewhat dubious oral traditions written down centuries after the events.

steps.title: Reject opposite relations
steps.content: Choice A says the knowledge is not supported by those traditions. Choice B says it is independent of them. Choice D says it is merely similar to them. None states that the knowledge comes from them.

steps.title: Choose origin
steps.content: Derived from means originating in, which fits knowledge based on dubious oral traditions recorded much later. The answer is C.
